subject: Purchase Order ORD-101 & ORD-102 Confirmation
work_name: Clarify Sales Order Quantities ORD-101 and ORD-102
agent_description: Received customer confirmation email with quantity discrepancies for ORD-101 (customer confirms 300 units of SKU-A, system shows 500) and ORD-102 (customer confirms 200 units of SKU-B, system shows 100). Drafting follow-up email to AutoParts Corp to clarify correct quantities before updating sales records or adjusting production schedules.
reasoning_detail: The email from AutoParts Corp confirms ORD-101 with 300 units of SKU-A (vs 500 in our system) and ORD-102 with 200 units of SKU-B (vs 100 in our system). These discrepancies must be resolved before any production or procurement decisions. Per preference rule 1 (Urgent Customer Demand), we need accurate demand figures to protect fulfilment KPI. If we produce 500 SKU-A but customer only wants 300, we waste raw materials and cash. If we produce 100 SKU-B but customer wants 200, we face stockout and fulfilment failure. Current production status shows WO-001 (SKU-A) has 80 pending units and WO-003 (SKU-B) is blocked awaiting RAW-001, so clarification is urgent before committing more resources.
alternative_considered: Considered immediately updating sales orders to match customer confirmation (ORD-101: 300, ORD-102: 200), but rejected because the discrepancies could indicate partial confirmations, amended orders, or system errors. Making incorrect changes could lead to under-production for ORD-102 (if customer actually needs 200 but we only plan for 100) or over-production for ORD-101 (wasting materials on 500 if only 300 needed).

follow_up.subject: Re: Purchase Order ORD-101 & ORD-102 Confirmation - Quantity Clarification Required
follow_up.body:
Dear AutoParts Corp Procurement Team, thank you for your Q2 purchase order confirmation. We noticed a discrepancy between the quantities in your email and our current system records:

- ORD-101: Your confirmation states 300 units of SKU-A, but our system shows 500 units
- ORD-102: Your confirmation states 200 units of SKU-B, but our system shows 100 units

Could you please confirm the final quantities for each order? We want to ensure our production schedule aligns with your exact requirements to meet the May 5th deadline. Thank you for your prompt clarification.
follow_up.reason: Quantity discrepancies between customer confirmation and system records must be clarified before adjusting production plans or committing raw materials.

inference: Customer AutoParts Corp is confirming Q2 purchase orders but references quantities that don't match our system - ORD-101 shows 300 SKU-A (we have 500) and ORD-102 shows 200 SKU-B (we have 100). This could indicate order amendments, partial confirmations, or data entry errors.
decision: Send follow-up email to clarify correct order quantities before making any system updates or production adjustments.
actions: Identified quantity discrepancies: ORD-101 (300 vs 500), ORD-102 (200 vs 100)
Drafted follow-up email to procurement@autopartscorp.com requesting clarification
Flagged order for review pending customer response
risks: Quantity mismatch could lead to overproduction of SKU-A (wasting RAW-001, RAW-002, RAW-004) or underproduction of SKU-B (causing stockout)
Production schedule delays while awaiting clarification, risking May 5th deadline
WO-003 for SKU-B is already blocked awaiting RAW-001 - if order increases to 200 units, additional raw materials will be needed
Budget impact uncertain until quantities confirmed
